ananke 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

Quantiles of a Density Estimate

Source: R/AllGenerics.R, R/statistics.R
quantile.Rd

Quantiles of a Density Estimate

Usage

# S4 method for CalibratedAges
quantile(
  x,
  probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25),
  na.rm = FALSE,
  ...,
  calendar = getOption("ananke.calendar")
)

# S4 method for ProxyRecord
quantile(x, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25), na.rm = FALSE, ...)

Arguments

x

A CalibratedAges object.

probs

A numeric vector of probabilities with values in \([0,1]\).

na.rm

A logical scalar: should NA values be stripped before the computation proceeds?

...

Currently not used.

calendar

A TimeScale object specifying the target calendar (see calendar()).

Value

A numeric

matrix containing the quantiles.

See also

Other statistics: hdr, mean(), median()

Author

N. Frerebeau

Examples

## Calibrate multiple dates
cal <- c14_calibrate(
  values = c(5000, 4500),
  errors = c(45, 35),
  names = c("X", "Y")
)

## Statistics
quantile(cal)
#>      0%   25%   50%   75%  100%
#> X -3984 -3876 -3778 -3729 -3545
#> Y -3984 -3283 -3211 -3145 -2917
median(cal)
#>     X     Y 
#> -3778 -3211 
mean(cal)
#>         X         Y 
#> -3792.679 -3211.160 

## Plot
plot(cal, calendar = CE())

## Need to set 'calendar'
abline(v = median(cal, calendar = CE()), lty = 2, col = "blue")
abline(v = mean(cal, calendar = CE()), lty = 2, col = "red")

On this page

ananke is a part of the tesselle project, a collection of packages for research and teaching in archaeology. Learn more at tesselle.org.

Developed by Nicolas Frerebeau, , .

Site built with pkgdown 2.0.9.
